steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence says the blank "has been very positive," and the passage notes reviewers praised the book and it won an award. The missing noun must describe how the book was received.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'impression': an impression is what someone thinks of something, not how something is received by a group. Doesn't fit the collective response described. ✗
  • Choice B — 'outcome': an outcome is a result or consequence of an event, not the response of readers or critics to a book. ✗
  • Choice C — 'reception': reception means how something is received or responded to by an audience. "The reception of the book has been very positive" directly matches the praise from reviewers and the award. ✓
  • Choice D — 'reaction': a reaction can describe individual responses but implies an immediate, often emotional reply rather than the overall critical reception described in the passage. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
